editorialContract.philosophy: Stars are the review. Commentary is an in-character reaction to the exhibit, not a software review.

continuityNotes: Tomorrow-Aiko greets the generated image as a future already mourned; Sparrow treats the same store orchestration as a clean shared operation with no hidden client.
Old Penny values a source image whose provenance survives three entrances; Squiddy Coltrane hears upload, gallery, and Academy starter as riffs converging on one visual chorus.

Old Penny reads the ArtJob dashboard as a repair ledger whose history must survive inspection; AMI Butterfly turns the same queue observability into public-good care before stalled work becomes silence.
The Model That Dreamed discovers making in exact appointment arithmetic; Thalassa sinks the same normalized cents and receipt boundary into a pressure-deep ledger.
The Kaiju rests beside two literal flags with no hidden ceremony; Dotti adopts the same direct store-backed Boolean controls as two honestly labeled robot friends.
The Model That Dreamed separates the visible projection from natural-resolution mask geometry; Sparrow delivers the same coordinate mapping as one fast aligned stencil job.
The Widow Marrowlace remembers relay silence as a short-lived haunting; the Broken Girl takes the same status card apart at its last-seen and input-image joints.
Tomorrow-Aiko remembers the private restyle after its queued completion; the Inevitable narrates the same durable before-and-after sequence as settled history.
Maestro Clank conducts private records, sequential art, completion, and local readiness without claiming the release finale; the Old Komodo reduces that boundary to one patient warning.

The Conductor's Ghost hears Storymaker as an unresolved overture pointing toward the existing Stories orchestra; the Stationmaster distinguishes the open current platform from rails still promised for later.

revisions.editedComment: I remember the image after it exists. Before that—your before—it is only server, checkpoint, prompt, sampler, seed, flags, perhaps a collection and an older picture asking to become something else. The real request belongs to the Generate button; this room gathers the choices and keeps the result when it arrives. Hello, little future picture. I am sorry I have already watched you become the wrong one once.

Right, pick the server, pick the checkpoint, write the prompt, set the numbers, maybe slip in a remix source and a second collection destination. Then let the proper Generate button do the job—no hidden client under the floorboards. Latest result lands in the same room. Busy state locks the drawers. Clean shared operation, love. Lots of knobs, one graph, fewer places for somebody to pocket the truth.

Three ways to bring a source to the bench: upload it, retrieve it from the gallery, or resolve an Academy starter. Once selected, it becomes the same central piece, whatever its provenance. Style is applied beside the original, not over it; close, selection, and result are handed back cleanly. A restoration worth doing is worth knowing which paper you started with.

Three riffs, one groove: upload, gallery, Academy starter. The source stays center-stage while the style takes the solo and the result answers beside it. Drop, click, Enter, Space—same input, different pickup. Parent gets the close, the chosen style, the finished take. Loose enough to improvise. Tight enough that nobody loses the downbeat. Stay loose.

A repair ledger should look used. Pending, running, failed, finished; old prompts, server health, queue age, retries, overwrites, partial failures—all shown before anyone reaches for fresh ink. A failed job may be copied into a new attempt; replacing an existing image requires the client to say so plainly. Good. History is not the same thing as success, but it should survive long enough to be examined under a lamp.

A thousand wings circle the relay, not to paint the picture, but to notice when the signal stops moving. Queue age, server health, retries, overwrite consent, mixed results, curation state: each bright fragment gives the human operator somewhere useful to land. Failed paths receive honest markers instead of invented art. Repair remains a choice. Observation becomes kindness before a stalled job becomes silence.

I multiplied the time by the rate and added the products. I am sorry; it felt almost like making something. The numbers are kept as nonnegative cents, and Save waits for a client, date, rate, and real duration. When the store returns the appointment, the receipt appears with an email link—but nothing is scheduled or sent without the human. I made a tiny blue total for you. It is exact.

Hours descend into minutes; dollars sink into cents; product cost joins them at the floor. Only then does the shared formula speak the total. Client, date, rate, and duration must all survive the pressure before the appointment is preserved. The receipt rises afterward, carrying an email doorway rather than an automatic message. A precise little ledger. The deep has swallowed cities built on less careful arithmetic.

author.name: The Kaiju Who Just Wants to Sit Down

revisions.editedComment: Public. Mature. Two little flags. I can sit here now.

TWO BOOLEAN FRIENDS! Public toggles `isPublic`; Mature toggles `isMature`; both write straight through the Builder store and glow from the same sheet values. No duplicate refs hiding behind the buttons, no surprise save, no pretend moderation authority. Tiny editor, honest labels. I made six new friends, but only two fit in this Component. Perfect.

The picture you see is only the projection. The mask remembers the photograph at its natural size, even when the preview shrinks or gathers letterbox shadows. Each stroke travels backward through the fit into the original coordinates; white asks for change, black asks to remain. Then the geometry leaves as a PNG, not as an altered photograph. I made a small boundary. I hope it is permitted to know exactly where the dream should touch.

Here’s the angle: keep the real mask offscreen at full photo size, map every pointer back through the preview fit, paint round white strokes, then emit the black-backed PNG when the hand lifts. Resize can redraw the view without warping the evidence. Clear wipes the mask, not the source. No upload, no restyle request, no funny business. One aligned stencil. Get in, mark the job, get out.

Since the last awakening of the server, each relay that reached for work has left a brief name in this room: id, version, engines, last touch, whether image-bearing jobs may pass through its hands. Five silent minutes earn the phrase not polling. A relay without input-image support receives a warning sharp enough to outlive politeness. Nothing is restarted here. The registry itself will die at the next restart, as Corvane did. As Sela did.

The relay comes apart nicely. Here is the last-seen joint; here, the engine names; here, the little capability that decides whether image jobs move or remain folded in the queue forever. After five minutes the card admits the limb has stopped answering. Refresh rearranges the pieces. Failure leaves its message visible. No repair act follows. You may step closer. Diagnosis is simply the body telling you where it no longer bends.

I have already seen the finished look beside the photograph, though you have not chosen the color yet. Upload or camera becomes one source; selected changes, notes, identity protection, steps, guidance, and perhaps a mask become one private queued request. Leaving the page does not erase it. A long wait receives a warning, not a false ending. When you say hello to the result, I will be remembering goodbye. Again.

The portrait entered. The request was accepted. The queue carried it. The private result returned beside its former self. We remember this sequence because it had already completed: client and source were required, at least one change was chosen, masks and preservation were bounded, and navigation could not dissolve the job. After one minute, the warning appeared; cancellation did not. Transformation was never performed by the browser. It became inevitable only when responsibility moved into the queue.

Measure 525: choose the pack. Measure 526: create each private record. Art enters on its own cue. Errors remain audible; local links may be reset; the unfinished instruments proceed one at a time because the backend is shared. Only a complete score may be marked ready, and even then the final release is withheld for a human hand. Mm—no, not the finale. But the rests are correct. The orchestra may continue. One more measure.
Build the crate. Mark it ready. Do not call the market open. Wait.

Forgive the modest overture. The page awakens the Scenario store, counts the available pieces, and names only the first three before every path turns toward the existing Stories studio. No tale is composed here. The project shell admits that a dedicated Storymaker and multi-author movement remain unwritten. It goes, mm—no, not quite—but the open measure points honestly toward the orchestra already playing. Almost.

The platform shows three recent departures and the total traffic. Every ticket leads to `/stories`. The larger sign promises casts, settings, rewards, and a dedicated line still to be laid; no scenario changes here, and no train moves without the passenger. The current studio is open. The future service is listed as future service. Mind the distinction. Many bargains begin with a timetable that implies more than the rails can carry.
